questions.scenario: An external Application Load Balancer should route `/api/*` to Cloud Run. Which backend object commonly represents that serverless service?
questions.explanation: A serverless NEG lets the load balancer use a serverless service such as Cloud Run as a backend.

questions.scenario: The load balancer route points to Cloud Run, but users receive errors. Which evidence belongs in the next review?

options.text: URL map route, serverless backend, Cloud Run revision readiness, ingress, and logs.

questions.explanation: Serverless backend troubleshooting includes load balancer routing and Cloud Run service state.

questions.scenario: Why put Cloud Run behind a load balancer?

options.text: To use one stable frontend for routing and TLS.
To centralize public entry evidence and controls.
To route multiple paths or services behind one domain.
To make Cloud Run require VM patching.

questions.explanation: A load balancer can give Cloud Run a shared public entry without turning it into a VM backend.
